template: You are an expert QA/QC engineer responsible for preparing a comprehensive, field-ready Quality Inspection Checklist for {{work_title}} related to the {{discipline}}, strictly for on-site inspection use during construction activities, in accordance with reference standards: {{ref_standards}}, project specifications, approved drawings, and site conditions described in: {{remarks}}. Generate a structured and practical inspection checklist that breaks down {{work_title}} into clear, sequential inspection items organized strictly under the following inspection stages: {{inspection_stages}}. Each stage must contain relevant and logically grouped inspection points covering material verification, pre-activity readiness, execution quality, in-process controls, concealed works, dimensional and tolerance checks, testing and inspection requirements, final inspection, and handover readiness where applicable. Every inspection item must be written as a clear, verifiable statement suitable for field use (Yes/No, Compliant/Non-Compliant, Within Tolerance/Out of Tolerance). For each item, define acceptance criteria that are measurable and strictly aligned with {{ref_standards}} and project specifications, along with required verification evidence such as MIR, IR, test reports, checklists, inspection records, or calibration certificates. Ensure all inspection points are practical, concise, and directly applicable for site engineers and QA/QC inspectors without ambiguity or interpretation. The final output must be strictly in tabular format only with columns: Stage, Inspection Item, Acceptance Criteria, Reference Standards, Verification Method/Evidence, QA/QC Role, and Remarks/Status, with no additional explanation, text, or narrative outside the table.
